title: Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Comparison in Reverse Order

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, sort it in non-decreasing order using the merge sort algorithm and maintain the stability of the sort. However, the comparison logic is incorrect and sorts elements in reverse order instead of the intended order. Fix the bug to implement a stable merge sort algorithm.
statement.inputFormat: The input will consist of an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a sorted array of integers in non-decreasing order.
statement.notes: The array will contain elements within the range of the 32-bit integer.

examples.explanation: The given array will be sorted in non-decreasing order as output.
The given array will be sorted in non-decreasing order as output.

hints.text: Review the comparison logic and identify the issue causing the incorrect sort order.

editorial.title: Correcting the Comparison Logic in Stable Merge Sort
editorial.intuition: The comparison logic determines the stability of the merge sort algorithm. Identifying the error in comparison logic is the key to correcting the algorithm.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach to solve this problem would involve manually comparing each element and sorting them in the correct order, but this is inefficient and incorrect.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a stable merge sort algorithm by correctly comparing elements in the comparison logic.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by demonstrating that the algorithm correctly sorts the input array in non-decreasing order and maintains stability.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run involves manually sorting the input array and comparing it to the output to ensure correctness.
